statements.impact_statement: This image uses wolfSSL FIPS 5.8.2 (NIST CMVP Certificate #4718) for all cryptographic operations. All crypto operations are FIPS 140-3 compliant. Non-FIPS algorithms are blocked at runtime.
statements.action_statement: No action required. Image is FIPS 140-3 compliant and passes all validation tests.

statements.impact_statement: OpenSSL 3.5.0 is a custom build from official source (Git tag openssl-3.5.0). This version provides excellent compatibility with Debian Trixie ecosystem (which uses OpenSSL 3.5.5) and wolfProvider 1.1.1. All cryptographic operations route through wolfSSL FIPS provider, not OpenSSL's built-in crypto.
statements.action_statement: Monitor OpenSSL 3.5.x security advisories. Update to latest 3.5.x stable release when available. Re-run validation suite after updates.

statements.impact_statement: Chromium is used for HTML to PDF rendering. Version is latest from Debian Trixie repository. Chromium security updates are applied via Debian package updates. TLS connections use system OpenSSL 3.5.0 which routes through wolfSSL FIPS provider.
statements.action_statement: Regularly update Chromium via 'apt-get update && apt-get upgrade chromium'. Monitor Debian security advisories. Chromium runs in sandboxed mode with limited network access.

statements.impact_statement: LibreOffice is used for Office document to PDF conversion. Version is latest from Debian Trixie repository. LibreOffice security updates are applied via Debian package updates. Document processing is isolated.
statements.action_statement: Regularly update LibreOffice via 'apt-get update && apt-get upgrade libreoffice-*'. Monitor Debian security advisories. Implement input validation and file size limits for uploaded documents.

statements.impact_statement: golang-fips is a Red Hat maintained fork of Go with FIPS crypto backend. It routes crypto/tls operations through OpenSSL via CGO. This is a build-time dependency only - the compiled Gotenberg binary is the runtime artifact.
statements.action_statement: Monitor golang-fips repository for updates. Rebuild Gotenberg when new golang-fips versions are released. Runtime crypto operations use wolfSSL FIPS, not Go's crypto library.

statements.impact_statement: wolfSSL FIPS 5.8.2 is a NIST CMVP validated cryptographic module (Certificate #4718). FIPS 140-3 validated modules undergo rigorous security testing. No modifications are permitted to the validated binary (FIPS requirement). Any vulnerability would require NIST re-validation.
statements.action_statement: Monitor NIST CMVP website for certificate status: https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/cryptographic-module-validation-program/certificate/4718. Monitor wolfSSL security advisories. Update only to new FIPS-validated versions when available.

statements.impact_statement: Gotenberg 8.26.0 requires zero patches for FIPS compliance. All cryptographic operations use standard OpenSSL EVP API which routes through wolfProvider to wolfSSL FIPS. Clean architecture ensures easier version updates and reduced maintenance burden.
statements.action_statement: Monitor Gotenberg releases: https://github.com/gotenberg/gotenberg/releases. Update to new versions as needed. Re-run validation suite after updates. FIPS compliance is maintained through OpenSSL provider layer.

statements.impact_statement: RESOLVED: Debian Trixie's curl now works correctly with OpenSSL 3.5.0. Previous versions used OpenSSL 3.0.19 which was incompatible with curl's requirement for OpenSSL 3.2.0+. Upgrading to OpenSSL 3.5.0 resolved this compatibility issue. Native curl functionality is now available.
statements.action_statement: No action required. curl works natively with OpenSSL 3.5.0. Demo scripts can now use curl instead of Python urllib workaround.

notes: All cryptographic operations use FIPS 140-3 validated wolfSSL module
Zero source code patches required for FIPS compliance
OpenSSL 3.5.0 custom build provides excellent Debian Trixie compatibility
curl compatibility resolved with OpenSSL 3.5.0 upgrade
Chromium and LibreOffice receive security updates via Debian
Regular security monitoring and updates recommended
FIPS POST (Power-On Self Test) executed on every container start
